name: Pork Chops with Crust of Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. You can use 1 teaspoon of dried tarragon instead of 2 teaspoons of fresh tarragon.
The pork chops should have the bone in and weigh about 10 oz each.
2. Sprinkle the pork chops with salt and pepper.
3. Melt 2 1/2 tablespoons of butter in a large heavy frying pan over medium-high heat.
Brown the chops on both sides, turning frequently so they do not stick.
4. Remove the chops from the pan and sprinkle with tarragon. Cover and keep warm.
5. Add another tablespoon of butter to the pan drippings and sauté onions for 3 minutes or until soft and golden.
6. Spread 2/3 of the onions in the bottom of a shallow casserole. Arrange the chops, tarragon-side down on top.
Sprinkle lightly with thyme and cover with onions.
7. Pour the wine into the pan, bring to a boil, add chicken stock, return to the boil,
and cook until the liquid is reduced to about 2/3 of a cup. Pour the mixture over the chops.
8. Combine the bread crumbs and cheese, spread over onions and chops.
Melt the remaining 2 tablespoons butter and drizzle it over the crumbs.
Bake at 400°F for 30 minutes. Serves 4.
